name: Make-Their-Own-Pizza
recipeIngredient: 16 oz. refrigerated biscuit or roll dough (2 rolls per child)
recipeInstructions:
1. In advance of the party, prepare the dough by rolling it out very flat between sheets of waxed paper. Place the flattened dough on a cookie sheet in the refrigerator.
2. When ready to bake, preheat the oven to the temperature specified on the dough package (usually 350-425°F).
3. Pour the spaghetti sauce into a bowl that can be easily reached by all participants.
4. Set out bowls of shredded mozzarella and Parmesan cheese, as well as any additional toppings you desire (e.g., sliced green peppers, grated carrots, etc.), on a serving platter.
5. Provide each child with two flattened dough rounds on separate paper plates.
6. Allow the children to assemble their own pizzas by spreading sauce over the dough, adding cheese and desired toppings using spoons.
7. Once assembled, carefully transfer the pizza creations onto prepared baking sheets.
8. Bake in the preheated oven until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
9. Remove from the oven when ready and serve immediately. Enjoy!
